methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pipedream is a Transportation-sector company at the Acquired stage, founded by Garrett McCurrach and Canon Reeves, having raised $22.4M in total capital. The broader Transportation sector contains five identifiable private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables identified. However, the peer set is highly heterogeneous in stage and scale, limiting direct comparability.

The Peer-Median valuation method is not applicable here. A disciplined search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers within Transportation yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category, falling below the minimum threshold of three observations required for a reliable median. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the wide dispersion in business models, capital intensity, and development timelines across the broader Transportation universe. Without a sufficiently tight peer cohort, this triangulation method cannot be responsibly executed.

The absence of applicable valuation methods constrains analysis. To establish a defensible range, additional approaches—such as discounted cash flow, sum-of-the-parts, or precedent transaction analysis within narrower subsets—would be required but are not supported by the provided data. The $22.4M raised provides a floor reference point but does not indicate fair value absent market comparables or cash flow visibility. Meaningful valuation synthesis is not possible under current data constraints.
